chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا عِنْدَ الْأَئِمَّةِ مِنْ سِلَاحِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ مَتَاعِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 38 – What is with the Imams<sup>asws</sup> from the weapons of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> and his<sup>saww</sup> chattels

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (46/ 3)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (46/ 3)
text: السِّلَاحُ مَوْضُوعٌ عِنْدَنَا مَدْفُوعٌ عَنْهُ لَوْ وُضِعَ عِنْدَ شَرِّ خَلْقِ اللَّهِ كَانَ خَيْرَهُمْ

لَقَدْ حَدَّثَنِي أَبِي أَنَّهُ حَيْثُ بَنَى بِالثَّقَفِيَّةِ وَ كَانَ قَدْ شُقَّ لَهُ فِي الْجِدَارِ فَنُجِّدَ الْبَيْتُ فَلَمَّا كَانَتْ صَبِيحَةُ عُرْسِهِ رَمَى بِبَصَرِهِ فَرَأَى حَذْوَهُ خَمْسَةَ عَشَرَ مِسْمَاراً فَفَزِعَ لِذَلِكَ وَ قَالَ لَهَا تَحَوَّلِي فَإِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ أَدْعُوَ مَوَالِيَّ فِي حَاجَةٍ فَكَشَطَهُ فَمَا مِنْهَا مِسْمَارٌ إِلَّا وَجَدَهُ مُصْرِفاً طَرَفَهُ عَنِ السَّيْفِ وَ مَا وَصَلَ إِلَيْهِ مِنْهَا شَيْ‏ءٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus Bin Abdul Rahman, from Muhammad Bin Hakeem,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ibrahim<sup>as</sup> (7<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>) having said: ‘The weapons are placed with us<sup>asws</sup>, defended from. Had these been placed with the most evil creature of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, he would have been the best of them.

My<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup> has narrated to me<sup>asws</sup> that he<sup>asws</sup>, when he built at Al-Sawafiyya, and it was so that for him<sup>asws</sup> there was a wall which had splits in it, so he<sup>asws</sup> renewed the room. So when it was the morning of his<sup>asws</sup> marriage, he<sup>asws</sup> looked around with his<sup>asws</sup> eyesight, and he<sup>asws</sup> saw fifteen nails sticking out. So he<sup>asws</sup> was anxious at that and said to her (the bride): ‘Transfer, for I<sup>asws</sup> want to invite my<sup>asws</sup> friends regarding a need’. So he<sup>asws</sup> scraped it, and there was no nail from these except that its end was bent from the sword, and nothing from these had arrived to it’.
en.sarwar: 6. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus ibn ‘Abd al-Raman from Muhammad ibn al-Hakim from abu Ibrahim (a.s.) who has said the following. “ The arms are kept with us. They are well protected. Even if they would placed with the worst of the creatures he would a good one of them. My father stated to me that when he married a lady from al-Thaqif the house was decorated for the wedding. Next morning he found fifteen nails drawn into that wall. He became very anxious about it and asked hid bridegroom to leave the house because he needed the handyman to do some work therein. When he examined the wall he found that all the nails had turned back from the sword and none of them had reached it.”
